title: LOW: No HSTS header (Strict-Transport-Security)
description:
## Location
src/server.rs, security_headers middleware (lines 62-82)

## Finding
The security_headers middleware sets X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, Referrer-Policy, and CSP, but does not set Strict-Transport-Security (HSTS). Broadside generates all actor URIs with https:// and expects to run behind TLS, but does not instruct browsers to enforce HTTPS-only connections.

## Impact
Without HSTS, a browser that first visits via HTTP (before redirect) is vulnerable to SSL-stripping MITM attacks. This is low severity because:
1. Broadside is typically behind a reverse proxy (nginx/caddy) that may set HSTS itself
2. The primary consumers are ActivityPub servers, not browsers
3. Browser traffic is limited to the index and profile pages

## Recommendation
Add Strict-Transport-Security header in the middleware, or document that the reverse proxy should set it. A reasonable value: `max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains`. Consider making this configurable since the server may run behind a proxy that already sets HSTS.
close_reason: Delegated to reverse proxy. The proxy owns TLS termination, so it sets HSTS. Caddy does this automatically; nginx example in README includes Strict-Transport-Security header. The finding itself noted broadside is typically behind a proxy that may set HSTS.
